data.content: Implementation plan (low-to-medium complexity): 1. Create a dedicated short-lived worktree and install dependencies with bun install --frozen-lockfile. 2. Add gpt-5.6-sol, gpt-5.6-terra, and gpt-5.6-luna at the front of the Codex static model catalog in src/lib/settings-agent-scan.ts. These exact runtime IDs were verified against Codex CLI 0.144.1 model/list and the current Codex manual. 3. Extend the canonical Codex identity parser in src/lib/agent-identity.ts so the three suffixes produce distinct Sol, Terra, and Luna flavor labels and retain version 5.6. 4. Update deterministic coverage in src/lib/__tests__/settings-scan.test.ts and src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity-matrix.test.ts, plus scripts/audit-extractors.ts and the identity contract documentation, so runtime IDs, labels, and canonical metadata remain aligned. 5. Add or extend adapter coverage proving each 5.6 model is passed to Codex unchanged while the existing runtime reasoning setting still emits model_reasoning_effort, using supported current values low through xhigh. No new setting surface is required because all three models support the full existing Codex effort enum. 6. Run focused tests and the extractor audit, then full bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test, and bun run build. Fix any failures as required by repository policy. 7. Commit in the worktree with Sneka attribution note, integrate and push to origin/main without a PR, then run the patch release workflow from clean synced main. Release preflight will preview commits since v0.14.16 before bun run release -- --patch --wait-for-artifacts. Risks: parser suffixes must not collapse the three UI labels; main can move between integration and release; release must stop on any gate or artifact verification failure. No child knots are needed.
